claim: Context grows unbounded in agents because each tool call appends its observation to the message history, causing exponential context accumulation that degrades model performance
actionable: Monitor context length throughout agent execution and implement context management strategies before hitting the pre-degradation threshold (~128K-200K tokens even when model supports 1M+)
source_title: Context Engineering for AI Agents with LangChain and Manus

claim: Context offloading via filesystem is more effective than vectorstore indexing for coding/agent tasks because it avoids indexing latency and is compatible with shell-based retrieval tools
actionable: Use filesystem and grep/glob tools for context retrieval instead of building vectorstores on-the-fly; save tool outputs to files and pass file paths back to the agent
source_title: Context Engineering for AI Agents with LangChain and Manus

claim: Compaction (reversible reduction) differs from summarization (irreversible); compaction keeps identifiers like file paths while externalizing large payloads, enabling on-demand retrieval without information loss
actionable: For each tool output, create a compact form containing only the unique identifier (file path, URL, query) and offload the full data; allow agents to retrieve via that identifier when needed
source_title: Context Engineering for AI Agents with LangChain and Manus

claim: Structured output schemas are superior to free-form summarization for producing consistent, high-recall summaries because they enforce which fields are always included
actionable: Define a schema with required fields (modified files, user goal, current progress) and constrain the summarization to fill those fields instead of letting the model generate arbitrary text
source_title: Context Engineering for AI Agents with LangChain and Manus

claim: Layered action spaces (function calling → sandbox utilities → APIs) reduce context confusion and allow offloading tool definitions without breaking KV cache, with atomic functions (read/write/shell/search) at the top layer
actionable: Keep 10-20 atomic shell functions in the function-calling layer and offload complex tools (format converters, MCP CLI, browser ops) to sandbox utilities accessible via shell; use Python APIs for heavyweight computation
source_title: Context Engineering for AI Agents with LangChain and Manus

claim: Keep the newest 2-3 tool calls in full detail after summarization to prevent style drift, ensuring the model has fresh examples of the tool-calling pattern
actionable: When applying irreversible summarization, preserve the last N (typically 3) tool calls/results in their full form rather than summarizing everything
source_title: Context Engineering for AI Agents with LangChain and Manus

claim: Sub-agents with shared context (same sandbox/filesystem) is preferable to communication-only patterns when the task requires access to full intermediate history, reducing redundant retrieval and token overhead
actionable: For complex workflows (deep research, multi-step analysis), use sub-agents that share the main agent's state via filesystem rather than communication-only patterns; for simple, isolated tasks use communication-only
source_title: Context Engineering for AI Agents with LangChain and Manus

claim: Use constraint decoding to enforce sub-agent output schemas, ensuring communication between main and sub-agents conforms to expected shapes and enabling reliable map-reduce patterns
actionable: Define output schema in the main agent, pass it to sub-agents, and use constraint decoding with a 'submit result' tool that validates output matches the schema before returning
source_title: Context Engineering for AI Agents with LangChain and Manus

claim: Don't over-engineer context management; the biggest improvements come from simplification and removing unnecessary tricks rather than adding more sophisticated layers
actionable: Regularly audit your agent architecture and remove complexity; trust the model more; measure whether each optimization actually improves speed, stability, or quality
source_title: Context Engineering for AI Agents with LangChain and Manus

claim: Use K/V cache-friendly provider infrastructure (Anthropic, Google, OpenAI) over self-hosted open models because distributed KV cache is hard to implement; costs can be cheaper even with larger models
actionable: When choosing a model provider for agents, prioritize those with solid distributed KV cache infrastructure and input caching features; factor in actual cost-per-token at scale
source_title: Context Engineering for AI Agents with LangChain and Manus

claim: Use task-level or step-level routing to select different models for different subtasks (coding→Claude, multimodal→Gemini, math→OpenAI) rather than committing to a single provider
actionable: Implement routing logic that selects the optimal model for each subtask; test and benchmark each model on your specific use cases; rotate models as new releases arrive
source_title: Context Engineering for AI Agents with LangChain and Manus

claim: Minimize the number of sub-agents to <5 (planner, executor, knowledge manager) instead of role-based division (designer agent, programmer agent) because communication overhead grows combinatorially
actionable: Avoid anthropomorphizing your agent architecture; use sub-agents only for distinct concerns (planning vs execution vs memory), not for role-based specialization
source_title: Context Engineering for AI Agents with LangChain and Manus

claim: For foundational agent design, focus on context engineering rather than fine-tuning/post-training because MCP's open-ended action space makes fixed-action-space RL impractical
actionable: Invest context management and tool design over custom model training; if you need RL, it requires building a foundation model yourself, which is beyond most application companies
source_title: Context Engineering for AI Agents with LangChain and Manus

claim: Adopt a planner agent with its own context window for plan generation rather than free-form to-do.md lists to reduce wasted turns and token usage on plan maintenance
actionable: Implement an agent-as-tool planner that generates structured plans once; main executor references the plan without constantly updating it
source_title: Context Engineering for AI Agents with LangChain and Manus

claim: Identify your model's pre-degradation context threshold empirically (~128K-200K even with 1M token limits) and use it as a trigger for context reduction, not the hard token limit
actionable: Benchmark your model's output quality at increasing context depths; set context reduction to activate when context approaches the threshold where you observe quality drops, not the hard limit
source_title: Context Engineering for AI Agents with LangChain and Manus

claim: Use line-based file formats (plain text, logs) for external state rather than markdown because models are over-trained on markdown and may generate excessive bullet points in responses
actionable: Store agent state in plain text with line-based structure for grep/readability; prefer less markdown to avoid style bleed into model outputs
source_title: Context Engineering for AI Agents with LangChain and Manus
